title: ui-leaf IPC Protocol — v1
description: Line-delimited JSON messages exchanged between the ui-leaf binary (stdout) and its caller (stdin). Every message carries version:"1". InboundConfig is the first line of stdin; all subsequent lines are InboundMessage.

Version1.description: Protocol version literal. A new $id hosts future breaking versions.

InboundConfig.description: First line of stdin: config object that spawns a mount. No type discriminator; identified by position in the stream.

view.description: View name (resolved relative to viewsRoot) or inline TSX source string.

viewsRoot.description: Absolute path to the directory containing named view files.

data.description: Initial data object passed to the view component as props (any JSON value).

mutations.description: Names of mutation handlers the view is allowed to call.

openBrowser.description: Whether to open a browser tab automatically on startup.

csp.description: Content Security Policy preset name or raw policy string.

heartbeatTimeoutMs.description: Milliseconds before a missing browser heartbeat closes the mount. Default: 5000.

startupGraceMs.description: Milliseconds to wait for a first browser connection before emitting disconnected.

InboundMutateResult.description: Caller's response to a mutate request: success path. Correlated by id.

id.description: Mutation request ID echoed back from the OutboundMutate message.

value.description: Return value from the mutation handler (any JSON value, including undefined/absent for void mutations).

InboundMutateError.description: Caller's response to a mutate request: error path. Correlated by id.

id.description: Mutation request ID echoed back from the OutboundMutate message.

message.description: Human-readable error message from the mutation handler.

InboundUpdate.description: Replace in-memory data and emit a data-updated SSE event to connected browser tabs.

data.description: New data object to pass to the view component as props (any JSON value, including null).

InboundView.description: Swap the view source on-the-fly; triggers a recompile and a view-swapped SSE reload.

source.description: Self-contained TSX source string. Relative imports are not supported; bare-package imports (react, react-dom) work via the internal alias plugin.

InboundPatch.description: Atomically replace both data and view source. If the compile fails, neither takes effect and the previous state is preserved.

data.description: New data object (any JSON value, including null).

InboundReopen.description: Re-invoke open(url) to launch a fresh browser tab at the same mount URL.

InboundClose.description: Terminate the mount cleanly (caller-initiated close). The binary emits OutboundClosed with reason:"caller" and exits 0.

InboundPing.description: Caller heartbeat. The binary silently acknowledges and keeps the mount alive. No reply is emitted.

InboundMessage.description: Any valid post-config inbound message (lines 2+ of stdin).

OutboundReady.description: Emitted once when the HTTP server is listening and the view URL is known. The caller should open a browser at this URL if openBrowser was false.

url.description: Full URL the view is accessible at, including the auth token fragment.

port.description: TCP port the server bound to.

OutboundMutate.description: Emitted when the view invokes a named mutation. The caller must respond with InboundMutateResult or InboundMutateError carrying the same id.

id.description: Unique per-mount mutation request ID for correlating the response.

name.description: Mutation handler name as declared in InboundConfig.mutations.

args.description: Arguments passed to the mutation handler by the view (any JSON value).

OutboundDisconnected.description: Browser tab heartbeat stopped; mount stays alive, waiting for a reconnect.

OutboundReconnected.description: Browser reconnected after a prior disconnected event.

OutboundClosed.description: Mount terminated. The binary exits after emitting this message. Exit code is 0 for caller/signal, 1 for error.

reason.description: Why the mount closed: caller sent close, OS signal received, or unrecoverable error.

OutboundError.description: Structured error output. Errors without a phase tag are fatal (exit 1); phase:"build" errors are non-fatal (previous view preserved).

phase.description: Error phase tag, if applicable. Omitted for general errors.

OutboundViewSwapped.description: Emitted by the binary after a successful view swap (triggered by InboundView or InboundPatch). The caller uses this to resolve a pending setView() or patch() call.

OutboundMessage.description: Any valid outbound message (stdout lines emitted by the binary).

SseDataUpdated.description: SSE event broadcast to all connected browser tabs when in-memory data is replaced (via InboundUpdate or InboundPatch). The browser view should re-render with the new data.

data.description: The new data object passed to the view as props (any JSON value, including null).

SseViewSwapped.description: SSE event broadcast to all connected browser tabs after a successful view recompile (via InboundView or InboundPatch). The browser performs a full page reload to pick up the new view bundle.

SseClosing.description: SSE event broadcast to all connected browser tabs immediately before the HTTP server stops. The browser can use this to show a 'mount closed' overlay.

reason.description: Why the mount is closing: caller sent close, OS signal received, or unrecoverable error.

SseMessage.description: Any valid SSE event payload broadcast on GET /events. SSE frames are unversioned (no version field); the event stream is a server-push channel, not part of the stdin/stdout IPC.
